name: retrieval-v0
description: Tiny hand-crafted fixture for the Phase 2.8 retrieval smoke benchmark. Chunks mimic a small auth/billing service so each query has obvious correct answers.

chunks.context: Top-level login HTTP handler in src/auth/login.ts. Validates credentials with Zod, looks up the user, verifies the password, and issues a session token.
Zod schema for login request bodies in src/auth/login.ts. Requires a valid email and a password of at least 8 characters.
Logout HTTP handler in src/auth/logout.ts. Pulls the bearer token off the request and revokes the matching session.
Session creation helper in src/auth/session.ts. Generates a UUID token, computes expiry from SESSION_TTL_MS, and writes a row to the sessions table.
Session revocation in src/auth/session.ts. Deletes the row matching the supplied token.
Password verification helper in src/auth/session.ts. Wraps bcrypt.compare against the user's stored hash.
Invoice creation in src/billing/invoice.ts. Sums line items into total cents and writes an 'open' invoice row.
Stripe webhook handler in src/billing/stripe-webhook.ts. Verifies the signature with STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET and marks invoices paid when invoice.paid arrives.
Generic retry-with-exponential-backoff helper in src/utils/retry.ts. Wraps an async function and retries up to opts.max times.
Pino-based structured logger in src/utils/logger.ts. Honors LOG_LEVEL and redacts secrets like Authorization headers, tokens, and passwords.
App configuration in src/config.ts. Reads DATABASE_URL and Stripe secrets from env, with a SESSION_TTL_MS default of 7 days.

queries.query: how does the login handler validate credentials
how do we create a session token
where do we sum invoice line items
